rulings.Case Title: UMPQUA BANK VS. XELAN PROP 1, LLC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order For Residual Proceeds Held By Receiver (Pursuant To Order Dated March 14, 2022 In Case No. Cgc 15-545655)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 16, 2022 line 1. THIRD PARTY DALE DUNCAN, MARTA MENDOZA Notice Of Motion For Order For Residual Proceeds Held By Receiver (Pursuant To Order Dated March 14, 2022 In Case No. Cgc 15-545655) is off calendar for December 16, 2022 to be re-noticed for February 9, 2023 by the moving party per the moving party's December 2, 2022 filing. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: BARBARA R. SIMON VS. IRINA IOANA D'AMATO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Order To Show Cause Re: Contempt And Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 16, 2022 line 5. PLAINTIFF BARBARA SIMON Motion For Order To Show Cause Re: Contempt And Sanctions is GRANTED as follows: Order to Show Cause re: Contempt shall issue and the hearing shall be set for January 27, 2023. Order and supporting affidavit shall personally be served on Defendant no later than December 23, 2022. Proof of service must be filed no later than December 28, 2023. Defendant shall file a response no later than January 18, 2023. Plaintiff may file a reply no later than January 23, 2023. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: HOMERISESF VS. DANA BETINIS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 16, 2022 line 7. DEFENDANT DANA BETINIS MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS Hearing Required. By no later than 9:00 a.m. on December 16, 2022 Plaintiff to file a declaration stating that Plaintiff is able to allege in good faith facts showing that either (1) 24 CFR 882.511(d)(2) doesn't apply to this tenancy or (2) Plaintiff complied with 24 CFR 882.511(d)(2). Declaration shall include the proposed factual allegation. Declaration shall be limited to the allegation above or should state that no such factual allegations may be made in good faith. The Court will further address the parties' request for a Settlement Protective Date jointly made on November 21, 2022 in light to this motion being dated November 21, 2022 and filed on November 22, 2022. The Court may reconsider its liberal policy of granting Settlement Protective Date continuances. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
